---
title: "20260407_Figure_Workshop"
author: "Yu Zhang"
date: "2026-04-07"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

```{r, include=FALSE}
library(tidyverse)
```

```{r}
set.seed(97) 
n_samples <- 50

# Simulating data for three groups with different means and variances
simulated_data <- data.frame(
  Group = factor(
    rep(c("Control", "Treatment A", "Treatment B"), each = n_samples),
    levels = c("Control", "Treatment A", "Treatment B") # Ordering the factors
  ),
  Expression = c(
    rnorm(n_samples, mean = 5.0, sd = 1.0),  # Control
    rnorm(n_samples, mean = 7.2, sd = 1.5),  # Treatment A
    rnorm(n_samples, mean = 8.8, sd = 1.2)   # Treatment B
  )
)

# Create the plot
pub_plot <- ggplot(simulated_data, aes(x = Group, y = Expression, fill = Group)) +
  
  # A. Violin plot for overall density/distribution (made slightly transparent)
  geom_violin(trim = FALSE, alpha = 0.3, color = NA) +
  
  # B. Boxplot for quartiles and median (narrowed to fit inside the violin)
  geom_boxplot(width = 0.2, color = "black", alpha = 0.8, outlier.shape = NA) +
  
  # C. Jittered raw data points to show individual subject spread
  geom_jitter(aes(color = Group), width = 0.15, size = 1.5, alpha = 0.6) +
  
  # D. Color palettes
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("#E69F00", "#56B4E9", "#009E73")) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("#D55E00", "#0072B2", "#009E73")) +
  
  # E. Labeling
  labs(
    title = "Gene Expression by Experimental Group",
    subtitle = "Response measured at 48 hours post-treatment",
    x = "Experimental Cohort",
    y = "Relative Expression Level"
  ) +
  
  # F. Apply a clean theme
  theme_classic(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none", # Removed legend since X-axis labels are sufficient
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5, size = 16),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 12, color = "gray30", margin = margin(b = 15)),
    axis.text.x = element_text(face = "bold", color = "black", size = 12),
    axis.text.y = element_text(color = "black", size = 11),
    axis.title.x = element_text(margin = margin(t = 12), face = "bold"),
    axis.title.y = element_text(margin = margin(r = 12), face = "bold"),
    axis.line = element_line(linewidth = 0.8, color = "black"),
    axis.ticks = element_line(linewidth = 0.8, color = "black")
  )
```

```{r}
# write.csv(simulated_data, "20260407_Violin_Plot_Random_Simulated_Data.csv", row.names = FALSE)
```
